reasoning:
Wesley So is ranked #8 in the FIDE Top 100 players list published on November 1, 2025, which is currently displayed on the Wikipedia page 'FIDE rankings'. 

1. For November 16, 2025: Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page follows a monthly update cycle corresponding to official FIDE lists. The page is very unlikely to be updated before December 1, meaning So's ranking will remain at 8, matching his November 9 rank.
2. For December 9, 2025: In early November 2025, Wesley So was knocked out of the FIDE World Cup in the second round by Titas Stremavicius (rated 2527). This major upset loss (approx. 10 rating points) will be reflected in the December 1 FIDE list. Since So was only 1-2 points ahead of Gukesh and Firouzja in the November list, this rating drop will almost certainly push him below rank 8.
3. For February 7, 2026: Wesley So is not participating in the upcoming 2025 Tata Steel Masters (January-February), which is a key opportunity to gain ranking points. While there is more time for variation, his absence from major events and the strong performance of young rivals suggest he will likely stay outside the top 8. Probability is higher than December simply to reflect increased uncertainty over a longer horizon.
